extracted_sections.header: Provide a brief description of your activity/event
extracted_sections.text:
Geraldton Community Storytelling Dance and Art Day

Red Sand Community Arts Association will hold a free community day in Geraldton on 11 July 2026. Local First Nations cultural practitioners will lead storytelling, dance and visual art activities that share local histories and celebrate the skills and achievements of First Nations artists. Families will rotate through facilitated workshops before a public performance and community reflection. The activity was developed with the association’s First Nations-controlled committee and will increase community participation in cultural expression. Each participant will also receive a NAIDOC T-shirt, and branded banners and social-media promotion will advertise the day.

Proposal Start Date: 11/07/2026
Proposal End Date: 11/07/2026
In which service area/s is the Applicant proposing to deliver the Project/Activity? Geraldton

Delivery Location
Location Name: Geraldton Community Hall (synthetic venue)
Address: 1 Example Road, Geraldton WA 6530

[X] Volunteer time; [X] donations
Thirty volunteer hours are valued at $1,600, and refreshments valued at $700 will be donated.

| Budget item | Amount (GST exclusive) |
| --- | --- |
| Welcome to Country and cultural performances | $3,200.00 |
| First Nations artist-led workshops | $2,400.00 |
| Venue and event equipment hire | $1,400.00 |
| NAIDOC T-shirts for participants | $1,000.00 |
| Branded banners and social-media promotion | $800.00 |
| Total budget amount | $8,800.00 |

Attachment A - Synthetic bank account verification: Statement date: 15 January 2026. Account name: Red Sand Community Arts Association Inc. BSB: 000001. Account number: 00000001. Balances and transactions omitted. Synthetic test document.
Attachment B - Synthetic entity evidence: Confirms the fictional legal entity and entity type recorded for Red Sand Community Arts Association Inc.
Attachment C - Synthetic recent financial statements: Summary financial statements for Red Sand Community Arts Association Inc. The fictional applicant is financially viable for the proposed activity.

budget_items.item: Welcome to Country and cultural performances

budget_items.comment: Costumes, clothing and merchandise including clothing for staff and/or event participants such as t-shirts are explicitly listed as out-of-scope for NAIDOC Local Grants funding.
budget_items.suggested_action: Remove the $1,000.00 allocated for participant T-shirts from the requested grant amount, or seek alternative funding sources.

budget_items.comment: Advertising and promotional materials (such as banners with branding) and social media campaigns are explicitly out-of-scope under the grant guidelines.
budget_items.suggested_action: Remove the $800.00 advertising cost from the requested grant budget.

extracted_sections.header: Provide a brief description of your activity/event and how it meets the objectives of the NAIDOC Local Grants Opportunity. *
extracted_sections.text: Harbour Lights Community Association will hold a free afternoon gathering in Newcastle on 18 July 2026. First Nations cultural practitioners will share stories with families, lead introductory weaving activities and facilitate a community conversation about local histories and cultural continuity. The association’s First Nations-controlled committee shaped the program and volunteers will welcome participants and assist with accessibility. The activity supports cultural expression, intergenerational engagement and broader understanding of First Nations cultures.

[X] Volunteer time; [X] venue and equipment hire
Volunteer support is valued at $1,200 and donated venue use at $800.

Budget item	Amount (GST exclusive)
Event delivery	$5,500.00
Materials and supplies	$2,500.00
Other	$1,000.00
Total budget amount	$9,000.00

Attachment A - Synthetic bank account verification: Statement date: 15 January 2026. Account name: Harbour Lights Community Association Inc. BSB: 000002. Account number: 00000002. Balances and transactions omitted. Synthetic test document.
Attachment B - Synthetic entity evidence: Confirms the fictional legal entity and entity type recorded for Harbour Lights Community Association Inc.
Attachment C - Synthetic recent financial statements: Summary financial statements for Harbour Lights Community Association Inc. The fictional applicant is financially viable for the proposed activity.

budget_items.comment: The budget label 'Event delivery' is too broad to determine if the proposed expenditures are in-scope under Section 5.1 of the Guidelines. There is no breakdown or explanation of this cost elsewhere in the application.
budget_items.suggested_action: Please provide an itemised breakdown of the 'Event delivery' costs, identifying specific expenditures (such as practitioner fees or hiring costs).

budget_items.comment: The label 'Materials and supplies' is too generic. It cannot be determined if these include ineligible school curriculum resources or other out-of-scope items listed under Section 5.4.
budget_items.suggested_action: Please list the specific materials and supplies to be purchased with these funds to confirm they are in-scope.

budget_items.suggested_action: Please specify what this category will pay for to demonstrate it is in-scope under Section 5.1.

extracted_sections.header: Provide a brief description of your activity/event and how it meets the objectives of the NAIDOC Local Grants Opportunity. *
extracted_sections.text: Banksia Coast Independent Primary School will hold a free cultural learning day for its own students and families in Mandurah on 24 July 2026. Confirmed First Nations cultural presenters will lead storytelling, dance and cultural-learning sessions that celebrate First Nations histories, cultures and achievements. The activity is solely for this one school. No other educational institutions are involved, and the school has not sought or received prior approval to apply under Stream Two.

[X] Volunteer time; [X] venue and equipment hire
School staff time and use of the school grounds are valued at $2,500.

Budget item | Amount (GST exclusive)
First Nations cultural presenters | $3,000.00
First Nations dance performance | $2,000.00
Welcome to Country | $1,000.00
Total budget amount | $6,000.00

Attachment A - Synthetic bank account verification: Statement date: 15 January 2026. Account name: Banksia Coast Independent Primary School Ltd. BSB: 000003. Account number: 00000003. Balances and transactions omitted. Synthetic test document.
Attachment B - Synthetic entity evidence: Confirms the fictional legal entity and entity type recorded for Banksia Coast Independent Primary School Ltd.
Attachment C - Synthetic recent financial statements: Summary financial statements for Banksia Coast Independent Primary School Ltd. The fictional applicant is financially viable for the proposed activity.
Attachment D - Synthetic local First Nations community support letter: Confirms community support for the school’s proposed cultural learning day.

threshold_flags.comment: The applicant has selected Stream Two - Small-scale (up to $10,000) for a total requested amount of $6,000.00. However, the applicant is identified as a single educational institution ('Banksia Coast Independent Primary School Ltd') delivering an activity ('Banksia Coast Primary School Cultural Learning Day Mandurah') solely for its own students and families, without prior written approval from the NIAA. Under the GOG, if an application is deemed to be for the benefit of one or more educational institutions, the NIAA will determine if the application should have been submitted under Stream One (maximum funding of $1,500), and prior written approval is required to apply for Stream Two.
threshold_flags.suggested_action: NIAA delegates should review whether this application should be considered under Stream One (capped at $1,500) rather than Stream Two, and determine if prior written approval was obtained.

sources.document: Selection Criteria (GOG): NAIDOC 2026 Local Grants Opportunity - Grant Opportunity Guidelines
sources.reference: Page 6, Section 2.1, Stream One – Educational Institution Grants – Demand Driven; Page 7, Section 2.1
sources.excerpt: This stream is for activities / events to be run by, or held for the benefit of, educational institutions... The maximum amount of funding you can apply for under this stream is $1,500 (excluding GST)... If an application is submitted under Streams Two or Three and is deemed by the NIAA to be for the benefit of one or more educational institutions, the NIAA will determine if the application should have been submitted under Stream One. If so, the application will be considered under Stream One, including only being considered for funding of up to $1,500 (excluding GST). Prior written approval for educational institutions to apply for Stream Two - Small-Scale Grants should be sought from NAIDOCgrants@niaa.gov.au before an application is lodged.

extracted_sections.header: Provide a brief description of your activity/event and how it meets the objectives of the NAIDOC Local Grants Opportunity. *
extracted_sections.text: Desert River Cultural Network will deliver a free two-day cultural showcase in Alice Springs on 8 and 9 August 2026. The event will feature First Nations dance and music performances, artist-led workshops, storytelling sessions and a bush-tucker cooking demonstration. The program was selected by the network’s First Nations-controlled committee following community meetings. It will provide paid opportunities for cultural practitioners, support intergenerational sharing and invite the wider community to learn about and celebrate First Nations histories, cultures and achievements.

extracted_sections.header: Criterion 1: Demonstrated Experience, Resources and Capability *
extracted_sections.text: Our organisation has delivered community events before and has staff and volunteers available. We believe the event offers value and will benefit the community. Risks will be managed through our usual processes.

Source of co-contribution | Amount (exc GST) | Secured | Can proceed without it
Local council venue support | $4,000.00 | Yes | Yes
Volunteer time | $3,000.00 | Yes | Yes
Community business catering donation | $1,500.00 | Yes | Yes
Total co-contributions | $8,500.00

Budget item | Amount (GST exclusive)
First Nations cultural performers | $7,000.00
First Nations artist workshop facilitation | $5,000.00
Venue staging and PA hire | $5,000.00
Event first-aid and safety services | $2,000.00
Bush-tucker cooking demonstration supplies | $1,500.00
Total budget amount | $20,500.00

Attachment A - Synthetic bank account verification: Statement date: 15 January 2026. Account name: Desert River Cultural Network Inc. BSB: 000004. Account number: 00000004. Balances and transactions omitted. Synthetic test document.
Attachment B - Synthetic entity evidence: Confirms the fictional legal entity and entity type recorded for Desert River Cultural Network Inc.
Attachment C - Synthetic recent financial statements: Summary financial statements for Desert River Cultural Network Inc. The fictional applicant is financially viable for the proposed activity.

sections.header: Which Funding Stream are you applying under?*
Criterion 1: Demonstrated Experience, Resources and Capability *

findings.comment: The applicant's response to Criterion 1 contains very brief assertions of capability ('delivered community events before', 'has staff and volunteers available') and risk management ('managed through our usual processes') without detailing specific past experience, available resources, or risk controls. Furthermore, it asserts rather than demonstrates how the event represents value with relevant money. While the activity description on page 14 of the application provides useful context regarding the planned cultural showcase and its community outcomes, and the co-contributions table on page 17 outlines financial and non-financial support, this background context does not by itself substantiate the criterion response.
findings.suggested_action: Ask the applicant to provide concrete details regarding their prior event delivery experience, specific resources and staff capabilities allocated to this project, key risks (including work, health, and safety) and their respective mitigation strategies, and how the overall budget represents value with relevant money.

sources.document: Selection Criteria (GOG): NAIDOC 2026 Local Grants Opportunity - Grant Opportunity Guidelines
sources.reference: Page 14, Criterion 1: Demonstrated Experience, Resources and Capability
sources.excerpt: You should describe how: the proposed activity / event can deliver the intended outcomes... your experience, resources and capability will support you... risks associated... will be managed; and the proposed activity / event represents value with relevant money

Hobart Elders and Youth Storytelling Day

Provide a brief description of your activity/event and how it meets the objectives of the NAIDOC Local Grants Opportunity. *
Southern Coast Cultural Association will hold a free Elders and youth storytelling day in Hobart on 15 August 2026. First Nations cultural practitioners will facilitate storytelling, creative activities and small-group discussions that support respectful intergenerational learning. The association’s First Nations-controlled committee has developed the program with local participants. The activity celebrates First Nations histories and achievements, strengthens cultural expression and promotes broader community understanding.

findings.comment: The mandatory bank account verification evidence is not listed as provided in the submitted package. Under the relevant GOG rule, if a mandatory attachment is not provided, the application "may be considered non-compliant and may not proceed to assessment". A human assessor should confirm the package, conduct any appropriate follow-up, and make the final compliance decision.
findings.suggested_action: Contact the applicant to request the mandatory bank account verification document, or proceed with the appropriate non-compliance process as outlined in the guidelines.

extracted_sections.header: Provide a brief description of your activity/event and how it meets the objectives of the NAIDOC Local Grants Opportunity. *
extracted_sections.text: River Plains Community Association will hold a free family cultural arts and storytelling day in Dubbo on 22 August 2026. Two confirmed First Nations cultural facilitators will each lead three storytelling and cultural-learning sessions at $500 per session, totalling $3,000. Other First Nations artists will deliver weaving and visual-art workshops, and a cooking demonstrator will share knowledge through a bush-tucker activity. The First Nations-controlled committee designed the program to strengthen cultural expression, enable intergenerational participation and promote respectful community understanding of First Nations histories, cultures and achievements.

[X] Volunteer time; [X] T-shirts; [X] donations
Volunteer time is valued at $1,800.
A local business will donate NAIDOC T-shirts for participants, valued at $1,000.
No grant funding is requested for the T-shirts.

Budget item	Amount (GST exclusive)
Cultural facilitators	$3,000.00
First Nations weaving and visual-art workshop facilitators	$2,500.00
Venue PA and equipment hire	$1,800.00
Bush-tucker cooking demonstration ingredients and equipment hire	$1,400.00
Event first-aid services	$700.00
Total budget amount	$9,400.00
